evidence: Boundary hubs in shared/root layers are mostly pass-through aliases rather than clear ownership seams (`src/types/generationRow.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/uiClassNames.ts`).

Overlay/dialog state contracts drift across peer components for the same interaction model (`open/onOpenChange` in `src/shared/components/ConfirmDialog.tsx` and `src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx`, but `isOpen/onClose` in `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/DescriptionModal.tsx`, plus `isInfoOpen/onInfoOpenChange` in `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx`).

Feature decomposition around gallery item actions is still broad: `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` coordinates selector state, quick-create flow, add actions, and navigation while `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts` aggregates unrelated UI concerns in one state hook.

Import-time bootstrap risk is reduced in `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` via lazy singleton creation, but this improvement is isolated and does not remove broader ownership blur in shared entry points.

Task creation modules re-declare large task-specific param bags plus near-identical validate/build/create flows (`createTask` + `TaskValidationError`) across `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, and `src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts`.

Multiple orchestrator payload builders replicate optional-field expansion patterns and legacy compatibility branching (`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload`, per-task optional spreads), increasing interface surface area without strong shared contracts.
Editor/lightbox hooks compose many thin adapters that mostly forward state and callbacks (`useInlineEditState`, `ImageLightbox`, `useVideoLightboxRenderModel`) with high indirection cost.

Video lightbox seam is spread across mode/env/shared/edit/render models (`VideoLightbox.tsx` + `hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` + layout conversion), requiring cross-module synchronization for single UI behavior changes.
Layout boundary depends on large contract objects passed through adapters (`toLightboxLayoutProps` + `LightboxLayout`), making handoff shape broad and difficult to reason about.

Task-details extraction seams remain distributed between components and utilities (`VideoTravelDetails.tsx`, `ImageGenerationDetails.tsx`, `taskParamsUtils.ts`) with repeated fallback chains at integration boundaries.

`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` builds layered callback/result adapters (`runCallback`, `handleAddToShotResult`, `handleAddToShotAdapter`, `toCallbackError`) that add local control-flow ceremony before core operations.
`DataFreshnessManager.ts` combines polling policy, fetch circuit-breaking, toast throttling, diagnostics, and error classification in one class with long branch-heavy methods.
Detail components use long local fallback chains and local coercion helpers (`VideoTravelDetails.tsx`, `ImageGenerationDetails.tsx`) that increase branch density and maintenance overhead.

Multiple assigned files are pure compatibility/pass-through layers with no behavior (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/utils/time-utils.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useContentResponsive.ts`, `src/types/generationRow.ts`).

Several short hooks/types carry long generated-style explanatory blocks and inline narration that restate obvious code paths (`src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/useTemporaryVisibility.ts`, `src/shared/lib/taskCreation/types.ts`, `src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useTextCase.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/useStableSkeletonVisibility.ts`).

findings.summary: High-traffic shared aliases flatten ownership boundaries across domains.
evidence: `src/types/generationRow.ts` is a root-level type gateway that re-exports both canonical and legacy DTO contracts, making migration layering globally visible.
`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` is a pure re-export surface that centralizes unrelated device signal and view-model exports.
`src/shared/lib/uiClassNames.ts` acts as a global utility choke point imported by many UI modules, encouraging broad shared coupling instead of feature-local ownership.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit domain/feature entry points and migrate imports away from root alias hubs; keep legacy DTO aliases in a dedicated compatibility module with a deprecation path.

findings.summary: Dialog/tooltip controllable-state APIs are inconsistent across sibling components.
evidence: `ConfirmDialog` and `InstallInstructionsModal` use `open/onOpenChange`, while `DescriptionModal` uses `isOpen/onClose` for the same controllable-overlay pattern.
`InfoTooltip` introduces another naming pair (`isInfoOpen/onInfoOpenChange`), increasing API shape variance for similar state ownership.
These components live across shared and page/feature layers, so inconsistency propagates into callers and weakens predictable change boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one overlay control contract (prefer `open` + `onOpenChange`) and apply it across shared/page components, with compatibility adapters only where migration is staged.

findings.summary: Media gallery item behavior is split across broad components/hooks with mixed ownership.
evidence: `ShotActions` consumes multiple grouped prop-bags (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`) and coordinates navigation, selector behavior, async add flows, and UI affordances in one component.
`useMediaGalleryItemState` manages star toggling, drag state, info tooltip state, shot selector state, and create-shot modal lifecycle in a single hook.
`InfoTooltip` relies on parent-managed open state contracts, reinforcing a broad parent ownership surface instead of narrower sub-feature ownership.
findings.suggestion: Refactor MediaGalleryItem into smaller sub-feature modules (selection, shot-association actions, metadata/info overlay) with localized state hooks and thinner parent orchestration.

findings.summary: Task creators duplicate wide param-bag + validator + payload-builder stacks across task types.
evidence: Each file defines its own large interface(s) plus bespoke `validate*` and `build*` functions before calling the same `createTask` path.
Optional-field spread patterns and legacy compatibility branches are reimplemented per task instead of using shared composition primitives.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared task-schema/payload composition layer (common validators, optional-field emitters, legacy-normalization helpers) and keep task modules focused on task-specific deltas only.

findings.summary: Video lightbox behavior requires coordinating too many intermediate seam models.
evidence: State is transformed through mode/env/shared/edit/render layers before reaching `LightboxLayout`, with many pass-through fields and callback plumbing.
A small behavior change (panel or controls behavior) crosses several files/contracts rather than one clear seam.
findings.suggestion: Collapse render contracts into fewer boundary objects (for example: `media`, `actions`, `panels`) and move conversion logic to one adapter near layout entry.

findings.summary: Inline edit state orchestration adds wrapper layers with mostly prop forwarding.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` composes many intermediate helper hooks (`useInlineEditEnvironment`, persistence, inpainting/magic, reposition/img2img sync) that mostly shuttle arguments and return grouped objects.
`InlineEditView` and `ImageLightbox` then forward large grouped state into UI components with minimal transformation.
findings.suggestion: Create a narrower domain model for edit actions/state and expose focused hooks per panel mode, reducing pass-through object width and wrapper count.

findings.summary: Shot editor callbacks use layered adapter/result wrappers that obscure core intent.
evidence: Callback flow is split across `runCallback`, per-action `*Result` functions, and adapter functions that convert result objects back to throws.
Core actions (add/create/delete) are wrapped in repeated boilerplate for context/logData packing and error shape conversion.
findings.suggestion: Use one consistent callback contract (either throw-based or result-based) end-to-end and extract shared telemetry wrapper once to cut local branching and duplicate glue.

findings.summary: DataFreshnessManager concentrates policy, UI notifications, and diagnostics in one branch-heavy class.
evidence: `DataFreshnessManager` owns polling interval heuristics, circuit-breaker state, toast throttling, error classification, and diagnostics in a single class.
`useTasks` directly couples query fetch behavior to this manager (`onFetchSuccess/onFetchFailure`), amplifying seam complexity for future policy changes.
findings.suggestion: Split polling policy, failure tracking, and notification concerns into separate collaborators and keep `useTasks` dependent on a narrower freshness policy interface.

findings.summary: Generation-task boundary mixes Result-style and throw-style failures across the same seam.
evidence: `getPrimaryTaskIdForGenerationResult` / `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerationsResult` return `OperationResult` values.
Bridge helpers (`getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`, `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations`) convert `!ok` into thrown errors, while `preloadGenerationTaskMappings` catches and logs then continues.
Callers of the same conceptual API must handle both result objects and exceptions depending on entrypoint, increasing integration branching.
findings.suggestion: Standardize the seam to one failure model end-to-end (prefer `OperationResult` at repository + bridge layers, with one adapter at UI boundary if needed), and make all bridge exports follow that single contract.

findings.summary: Generation transformers emit divergent media fields for timeline vs gallery consumers.
evidence: `transformGeneration` computes `thumbUrl` via fallback logic and derives URL identity fields.
`transformForTimeline` sets `thumbUrl: genData.location` directly and does not reuse the thumbnail extraction logic.
This creates boundary drift for consumers sharing `GenerationRow`-like data, forcing downstream code to tolerate inconsistent media metadata depending on fetch path.
findings.suggestion: Route both transform paths through shared field builders (thumbnail/content/prompt normalization) so timeline and gallery hand off a consistent object contract.

findings.summary: Invalidation API requires `reason` but drops it entirely during execution.
evidence: `InvalidationOptions` and `VariantInvalidationOptions` both require `reason`.
Neither `performInvalidation`, `runInvalidationLifecycle`, nor `invalidateVariantChange` uses or forwards `reason`.
In contrast, adjacent preload APIs explicitly propagate context into error callbacks (`onError(context, error, logData)`), showing a clearer seam.
findings.suggestion: Either remove `reason` from the public invalidation contract or propagate it into tracing/log hooks so callers get a truthful, useful boundary API.

findings.summary: Pending clip action docs describe typed operations that the exported type does not encode.
evidence: The `consumePendingJoinClips` comment says each item is `{ type: 'fill' | 'append', clip }`.
`PendingClipAction` only contains `{ clip: VideoClip }`, and fill/append is implicitly recomputed later in `applyPendingClipActions`.
This blurs handoff intent between producer and consumer layers and makes the action payload under-specified for future integrations.
findings.suggestion: Align implementation and contract: either add explicit `type` to `PendingClipAction` and honor it in apply logic, or update docs and naming to reflect that actions are raw clip payloads only.

findings.summary: Timeline position update path contains dead locals and rethrow-only branches
evidence: `useTimelinePositions` declares `const _queryClient = useQueryClient();` but never uses it.
`unmatchedChangeIds` is populated in the DB update loop but never read after collection.
`const isTimeout = isTimelineWriteTimeoutError(error);` is assigned in catch and never used.
Inner `try/catch` around `persistTimelineFrameBatch` only rethrows the same error, adding branch noise without behavior change.
findings.suggestion: Remove unused locals (`_queryClient`, `unmatchedChangeIds`, `isTimeout` unless acted on) and collapse rethrow-only nested catch blocks. If unmatched IDs are important, surface them via `handleError` logData or toast; otherwise delete the tracking.

findings.summary: Default events array recreates listeners on each render
evidence: `useClickOutside` defaults `events` with an inline array literal (`events = ['mousedown']`).
The listener effect depends on `events`, so with omitted options this default array identity changes per render and forces unnecessary teardown/re-subscription.
findings.suggestion: Hoist a module-level constant like `const DEFAULT_EVENTS: EventType[] = ['mousedown'];` and use it for the default, or memoize normalized options before the effect.

findings.summary: Compatibility wrappers accumulate pass-through indirection without clear retirement
evidence: `time-utils.ts` is only a backward-compat re-export surface with no local behavior.
`useContentResponsive` is a deprecated alias that directly exports `useViewportResponsive`.
`generationRow.ts` is only canonical/legacy type forwarding aliases.
`generation/index.ts` is an index-only pass-through export file for two subcomponents.
findings.suggestion: Pick canonical import paths, run a codemod to update consumers, and remove or sharply limit compatibility shims. Add lint/import rules to prevent new usage of deprecated alias modules.

findings.summary: Verbose narrative comments/docblocks restate obvious logic across simple files
evidence: Large file headers and multi-example docblocks are used on straightforward hooks/types with little hidden behavior.
Inline step-by-step comments frequently narrate obvious statements (e.g., timeout clear/set flows and simple boolean toggles) rather than documenting non-obvious constraints.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to rationale/constraints only, remove explanatory restatements of obvious lines, and move usage examples to dedicated docs or tests where needed.

findings.summary: `shared/components` blends cross-cutting primitives with feature-specific workflow components.
evidence: `AuthGate` is a generic auth-loading gate, while `BatchDropZone` implements specialized batch grid drop semantics (generation/file drag types, frame-position insertion, optimistic skeleton logic).
This mixes architectural layers in one package and makes ownership unclear for future edits.
findings.suggestion: Move workflow-specific components (for example batch/prompt editing UIs) into feature/domain directories and keep `src/shared/components` limited to cross-feature primitives and thin reusable wrappers.

findings.summary: Deprecated hook naming remains part of active shared package surface.
evidence: `useContentResponsive.ts` defines `useViewportResponsive` but still exports `useContentResponsive` as deprecated alias.
The test file is still named and structured around `useContentResponsive`, reinforcing the legacy API boundary.
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration by renaming/removing the deprecated alias file and test entrypoint, then keep one canonical hook identity under a domain-consistent path.

findings.summary: Shared package uses inconsistent test colocation patterns across neighboring areas.
evidence: Hooks place tests under `__tests__` subdirectory, while components use adjacent `*.test.tsx` in the component root.
Inconsistent package layout increases lookup cost and weakens directory boundary predictability.
findings.suggestion: Standardize one test placement convention per shared package boundary (for example colocated `*.test.*` or dedicated `__tests__` folders) and apply it consistently.
